currentExperiment.focus: Open the Phase Three contained Elysia host passage: prove inside the mini-runtime lab whether a real Elysia app/mount/request layer can wrap or delegate into the contained server runtime boundary while preserving host, oRPC if crossed, RAWR runtime, harness/boundary, telemetry, and control-plane observation layers without claiming production HTTP readiness. Keep production migration readiness, production host lifecycle, durable Inngest semantics, live HyperDX product visibility, RuntimeCatalog persistence, native host telemetry/error mapping, public API/DX law, and final structure/Nx/generator ratchet fenced.

entries.source: Runtime spec section 9.2
entries.oracle: ExecutionDescriptorRef rejects optional-field bags and under-identified async step refs.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9 and 12
entries.oracle: RAWR service/plugin executable terminals expose .effect(...) only, reject .handler(...) or Promise bodies, and reject raw generator yields outside the RAWR Effect facade.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 5, 9, and 25
entries.oracle: The lab @rawr/sdk/effect facade is backed by real effect@3.21.2 while the public authoring surface rejects raw runtime constructors and the unverified Effect.pipe spelling.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 11.8 and 12.5
entries.oracle: The mini runtime resolves a registry boundary and supplies runtime-bound clients through invocation context; service calls still require .withInvocation(...).

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9.3 and 15.7
entries.oracle: Portable artifacts carry descriptor refs and not executable closures or descriptor tables.

entries.source: Runtime spec section 13.4
entries.oracle: Provider build returns ProviderEffectPlan, not Promise or CompiledExecutionPlan.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 13 and 24
entries.oracle: Profile-selected providers must cover non-optional provider resource requirements before boot, scoped by resource id, role, lifetime, and instance.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 17, 18, and 24
entries.oracle: Real effect@3.21.2 executes Effect.gen, root pipe, value .pipe, Exit/Cause failure modes, interruption, scoped acquire/release, finalizers, and a process-owned ManagedRuntime wrapper.

entries.source: Runtime spec section 14
entries.oracle: Real Effect Queue, PubSub, Ref, Deferred, Schedule, and Stream work behind the lab's RAWR-owned process-local resource probe without implying final resource API shape, durable async state, or domain truth.

entries.source: Runtime spec RuntimeSchema import surface and boundary validation claims
entries.oracle: TypeBox schemas provide static inference and runtime validation only after RAWR adapts them through RuntimeSchema.

entries.source: Runtime spec server-plugin lowering claims and oRPC boundary shape
entries.oracle: Native oRPC contracts and handlers are constructible as vendor boundary shape smoke artifacts; RAWR adapter compatibility remains unproven until a RAWR adapter consumes them. RAWR .effect(...) is proven only in the SDK authoring lane, not through a fake oRPC negative.

entries.source: Runtime spec async host handoff claims and Inngest boundary shape
entries.oracle: Inngest client/function/Bun serve handoff shapes are constructible, while durable scheduling semantics remain outside this lab proof.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 12, 13, 15, and 24
entries.oracle: Explicit lab declarations, including explicit async descriptors and cold server route factories, derive normalized graph artifacts, descriptor refs/table inputs, service binding plans, surface plans, route descriptors, dispatcher descriptor inventory, explicit dispatcher operation inventory, async owner-to-step membership artifacts, refs-only portable artifacts, compiled process plan, provider dependency graph, registry input, harness placeholders, scoped bootgraph module input, diagnostics, and bootgraph input without executing descriptor bodies, mounting hosts, or lowering providers.

entries.source: Runtime spec WorkflowDispatcherDescriptor and dispatcher integration requirements
entries.oracle: Explicit lab dispatcher derivation inputs preserve workflow refs and declared operation inventory, keep workflow refs inert when operations are absent, and diagnose operations that target undeclared workflows without deciding public dispatcher API, explicit-vs-ambient access policy, live WorkflowDispatcher materialization, or async provider acquisition.

entries.source: Runtime spec async workflow/schedule/consumer metadata and FunctionBundle lowering prerequisites
entries.oracle: Explicit lab async step refs declare exactly one workflow, schedule, or consumer owner plus a step id; widened invalid owners and duplicate owner/step memberships are diagnosed; derivation preserves membership artifacts without executing descriptor bodies, deciding public membership authoring syntax, durable scheduling, retry/idempotency, or native Inngest lowering.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 5, 11, 22, and 23
entries.oracle: Lab-local cold server route factories produce server route descriptors, execution refs, descriptor table inputs, surface runtime plans, and refs-only portable artifacts without executing route bodies, mounting Elysia/oRPC, importing production code, or treating explicit route refs as derivation proof; failed factories, invalid route paths, unsafe import markers, duplicate route identities, and explicit server refs without route factory coverage remain diagnostic negative space.

entries.source: Runtime spec bootgraph, provisioning, RuntimeCatalog, diagnostics, and finalization record requirements
entries.oracle: Fake boot modules start after dependencies, roll back only started modules on failure, finalize in reverse order while continuing after finalizer failure, and record redacted startup/rollback/finalization/catalog entries without live handles; compiler bootgraph input preserves provider resource, lifetime, role, and instance identity.

entries.source: Runtime spec service binding cache and RuntimeResourceAccess boundary requirements
entries.oracle: The mini-runtime cache validates explicit lab ServiceBindingPlan dependency inputs as an acyclic graph before cache construction, rejects missing, ambiguous, and cyclic dependency refs before client construction, materializes dependencies before dependents, lazily reuses bindings for identical construction-time process/role/surface/capability/service/config/dependency inputs, excludes invocation from cache identity, and exposes only sanctioned runtime resource/optional resource/telemetry/diagnostic/topology access methods.

entries.source: Runtime spec descriptor table, registry, and process runtime lifecycle
entries.oracle: The mini runtime assembles descriptor table and registry from refs, checks full ref identity, and invokes descriptors through runtime-owned Effect access.

entries.source: Runtime spec server/internal/async adapter lowering lifecycle
entries.oracle: Instrumented fake server and async host adapters accept only their boundary-specific descriptor refs and delegate to ProcessExecutionRuntime instead of executing descriptors directly.

entries.source: Runtime spec server/internal/async adapter lowering lifecycle
entries.oracle: The compiler emits lab-local native-shaped server callback and async step bridge payloads from pre-derived route descriptors and async owner-to-step refs; payload constructors and adapters preserve full ref identity, reject widened wrong-boundary or mismatched route/owner metadata before runtime invocation, avoid executable descriptors in payloads, and delegate to ProcessExecutionRuntime.

entries.source: Runtime spec harness mounting lifecycle and adapter lowering prerequisites
entries.oracle: Lab-local server and async started harnesses consume only adapter lowering payloads, expose route/step invocation callbacks, preserve full payload ref identity, reject raw descriptors and compiler plans before invocation, record start/invoke/stop lifecycle events, reject stopped invocations before adapter delegation, and delegate through ProcessExecutionRuntime.

entries.source: Runtime spec lifecycle/deployment handoff boundary
entries.oracle: Deployment handoff carries coherent portable artifacts and compiled process plans without descriptor tables, executable closures, live runtime handles, runtime access, or raw secret fields; app id mismatches and widened bad values are rejected at runtime, and object-literal live handles fail the negative type fixture.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P0
entries.oracle: Final workflow/schedule/consumer membership metadata channel remains unresolved; the lab proves explicit owner-to-step artifacts only, not public authoring syntax or durable async semantics.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P1
entries.oracle: ProviderEffectPlan remains opaque until acquire/release, telemetry, error, and lowering fields are locked.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 17, 18, 24, and 25.11
entries.oracle: One process-owned Effect managed runtime, internal EffectRuntimeAccess, Layer/Scope construction, and disposal/finalization remain runtime-substrate work, not public authoring.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 14 and 25.10
entries.oracle: Process queue, pubsub, cache, and concurrency resources must be local runtime resource contracts and must not imply durable async or domain truth semantics.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 13, 17, 23, and 24
entries.oracle: Lab-internal ProviderEffectPlan acquire/release hooks lower through RAWR-owned mini bootgraph/provisioning modules with real Effect execution, provider dependency graph diagnostics fail closed before provisioning, graph edges drive dependency ordering, rollback/release runs on failed acquire, finalization runs in reverse, release failures are recorded, provider identity is preserved, and catalog records are redacted.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 23, 24, and 25
entries.oracle: Contained executable/provider boundary policy records preserve exact execution/provider boundary kind, timeout metadata, retry-attempt declaration without retry scheduling, AbortSignal interruption propagation and classification, Effect Exit/Cause success/failure/defect/interruption classification, record-only telemetry metadata, redacted attributes, adapter-inherited runtime policy records, and provider acquire/release policy records. Final public policy API/DX, production retry/backoff, durable async semantics, HyperDX export/query, production host mapping, and catalog persistence remain fenced.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 23, 24, and 25; residual from audit.p1.effect-boundary-policy-matrix
entries.oracle: Final public boundary policy API/DX, production retry/backoff, timeout enforcement, durable async policy, native host error mapping, HyperDX export/query, product observability policy, catalog persistence, provider refresh policy, config precedence, platform secret stores, and arbitrary free-form diagnostic string redaction remain unresolved beyond the contained record-only policy proof.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 5, 9, and 25
entries.oracle: Curated Effect composition helpers must be selected deliberately, with root pipe or value .pipe(...) spelling and no public raw runtime constructors.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P1
entries.oracle: Final dispatcher access policy remains unresolved; the lab proves explicit operation inventory on dispatcher descriptors only, not whether public routes opt in, receive an ambient facade, or use a final SDK/DX shape.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P1
entries.oracle: The lab proves a narrow sanctioned access facade only; final public RuntimeResourceAccess method law remains unaccepted architecture.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P2
entries.oracle: Final public route import-safety law, production route module topology, native Elysia adapter lowering, OpenAPI publication, and production HTTP request path remain unresolved; the lab proves contained cold route factory artifacts and a contained oRPC Fetch request boundary only.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 12, 20, 21, and 24
entries.oracle: Contained native-shaped callback payload lowering, first mini harness mounting, and a contained oRPC Fetch request boundary are proven, but production host callbacks, Elysia mounting, native host error mapping, and final boundary policy remain unresolved.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 12, 19, 20, 21, and 24
entries.oracle: Contained async bridge payload lowering and a contained Inngest-facing Bun serve/function/step path are proven, but production Inngest worker mounting, durable scheduling, retry/idempotency, run history, and final async host policy remain unresolved.

entries.source: Runtime spec harness mounting lifecycle
entries.oracle: First contained server/async mini harnesses, a contained oRPC Fetch request boundary, and a contained Inngest-facing Bun serve/function/step boundary are proven, but production Elysia/oRPC/Inngest mounting, deployment wiring, full StartedHarness lifecycle, other host kinds, durable async semantics, and final boundary policy remain unresolved.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P2
entries.oracle: Initial resource/provider catalog is planning input only and does not assert final ids.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 13, 24, and 25; deployment spec runtime handoff sections
entries.oracle: Lab-contained provider config validates through RuntimeSchema before provider build/acquire, invalid config fails closed with diagnostic-safe errors, module/provider-id config snapshots and provisioning trace attributes are redacted, acquire/release failure records do not leak config secrets or live handles, and production config precedence/secret stores stay fenced.

entries.source: Phase Two claim ledger rows p2.compiler.provider-coverage, p2.provider.config-secret, p2.effect.boundary-policy, and p2.process-runtime.binding-access; runtime spec sections 13, 17, 23, 24, and 25
entries.oracle: A scenario provider set validates config, lowers provider acquire/release through a shared runtime-owned EffectRuntimeAccess into the mini bootgraph, exposes provisioned provider values only through sanctioned runtime resource access for a ProcessExecutionRuntime descriptor, records provider and executable boundary policies, and keeps raw config secrets/live handles out of catalog, trace, runtime-access observation, and process events. Final ProviderEffectPlan shape, production config precedence, platform secret stores, provider refresh/retry scheduling, public policy API/DX, RuntimeResourceAccess method law, native host error mapping, and production migration readiness remain fenced.

entries.source: Phase Two claim ledger rows p2.server.orpc-live-boundary and p2.server.elysia-mount; runtime spec sections 11, 12, 20, 21, 23, and 24
entries.oracle: A lab-contained @orpc/server/fetch RPCHandler receives a real Fetch Request, matches the /rpc prefix, assembles invocation context server-side from JSON-shaped request data, delegates only through a server adapter payload and started mini server harness into ProcessExecutionRuntime, preserves route/ref identity across boundary, adapter, harness, and runtime records, rejects unmatched paths before harness invocation, and keeps request/context/resource secrets out of response and observation records. Elysia mounting, production HTTP serving, OpenAPI/product API policy, final route topology/import-safety law, auth/logging semantics, native host error mapping, and production migration readiness remain fenced.

entries.source: Phase Two claim ledger rows p2.async.inngest-handoff, p2.async.callback-step, and p2.async.durable-residual; runtime spec sections 12, 19, 20, 21, 23, and 24
entries.oracle: A lab-contained inngest/bun serve handler receives a real Fetch Request, routes to a real Inngest.createFunction handler by absolute function id, crosses step.run with the stable async step id, delegates only through an async bridge payload and started mini async harness into ProcessExecutionRuntime, preserves owner/ref/step identity across boundary, adapter, harness, and runtime records, rejects unknown function ids before harness invocation, and keeps event/resource/execution secrets out of response and observation records. Durable scheduling, retry, replay, idempotency, run history, production worker topology, final async membership syntax, dispatcher public DX, product async policy, and production migration readiness remain fenced.

entries.source: Phase Two claim ledger rows p2.telemetry.redacted-records, p2.hyperdx.provider-proof, and p2.catalog.control-plane; runtime spec sections 22, 23.3, 23.5, 24, and 29
entries.oracle: A lab-contained Phase Two scenario composes provider provisioning/finalization records, oRPC server boundary records, Inngest async boundary records, process-runtime events, in-memory catalog records, injected-fetch OTLP export, and a migration/control-plane observation packet. The focused gate proves records are redacted before export, live handles and raw secrets are absent from OTLP payloads/export bodies/packet summaries, HyperDX-shaped export uses the explicit /v1/traces endpoint, packet summaries do not copy OTLP payload or export response bodies, and placement remains candidate-only. Product dashboards, query semantics, alerting, retention, production OpenTelemetry bootstrap, RuntimeCatalog persistence, control-plane topology, durable async run history, native host telemetry/error mapping, platform secret-store precedence, arbitrary DLP, and production migration readiness remain fenced.

entries.source: Phase Two claim ledger row p2.integrated.rehearsal; runtime spec sections 9, 12, 15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, and 29
entries.oracle: A lab-contained integrated rehearsal derives and compiles the fixture spine, preserves refs-only portable artifacts, emits adapter lowering payloads from derived route/async identity, provisions provider modules through the mini bootgraph, mounts contained oRPC server and Inngest async boundaries from compiled artifacts, delegates both boundaries through ProcessExecutionRuntime, finalizes the provider bootgraph, projects provider/server/async/catalog records into redacted telemetry, exports a stable OTLP payload through injected fetch, and creates a non-persistent migration/control-plane packet. A focused falsifier matrix keeps child proofs independently falsifiable by checking invalid provider config fails before build without secret leakage, unmatched oRPC paths do not invoke the harness, unknown Inngest function ids do not invoke the async harness, and telemetry run-id drift is rejected. This is integrated contained proof and migration-decision evidence only; it does not prove production migration readiness, Elysia, production HTTP/worker hosting, durable async semantics, product observability, RuntimeCatalog persistence, control-plane topology, native host telemetry/error mapping, platform secret-store precedence, arbitrary DLP, final public API/DX, or Phase Three topology/Nx/generator readiness.

entries.source: DRA research program workflow semantic documentation harvest
entries.oracle: High-signal runtime JSDoc/comments were harvested as migration/documentation substrate only; comment presence is not proof, runtime authority, final public API authority, or production readiness.

entries.source: Phase Two program workstream child 8 closeout and post-Phase-Two reframe handoff
entries.oracle: Phase Two closeout reconciles child workstream reports, proof manifest entries, diagnostic/spine/focus status, residuals, migration-decision evidence, and historical Phase Three structure/Nx inputs. Closeout itself adds no runtime behavior proof, does not authorize production migration, does not prove live runtime passage, and does not implement Phase Three final structure, Nx enforcement boundaries, Nx generators, generator idempotency, or ratchet/lock mechanics. Immediate next-phase sequencing is now governed by the post-Phase-Two reframe handoff.

entries.source: Phase Three program workstream and DRA workflow
entries.oracle: The Phase Three program workstream document and DRA workflow define the approved recursive coordination object for live-runtime-passage investigation inside the mini-runtime lab, including authority order, child-workstream lifecycle, proof ceilings, artifacts, review loops, residual routing, and closeout criteria. Phase Three opened after explicit user approval on 2026-05-01, and child 1 is closed as coordination-only scope/claim ledger. This entry adds no runtime behavior proof, does not open production migration, does not prove production readiness, and does not implement final structure/Nx/generator ratchet.

entries.source: Phase Three child 2 started process assembly plus stop/finalization passage
entries.oracle: A lab-contained started runtime passage derives and compiles fixture declarations, provisions provider resources through the mini bootgraph, assembles one instrumented ProcessExecutionRuntime, mounts contained oRPC Fetch and Inngest Bun serve/function/step boundaries, invokes both through the runtime, projects safe observation/control-plane evidence, stops server/async harnesses, finalizes provider/runtime resources, and rejects post-stop boundary invocations before runtime delegation. The oracle records post-stop oRPC failure by HTTP error, post-stop Inngest failure by protocol-native StepError in the step response and failure-classified boundary record despite HTTP 206, stopped harness records, unchanged runtime invocation count after stop, explicit runtime disposal evidence, redacted OTLP-shaped export, and candidate-only migration packet summaries. This remains contained simulation proof and does not imply production migration readiness, durable async semantics, product HyperDX observability, RuntimeCatalog persistence, native host telemetry/error mapping, final public API/DX law, or final structure/Nx/generator ratchet.

entries.source: Phase Three child 3 native boundary observation and failure semantics ledger
entries.oracle: Closed semantics ledger for native boundary failure and observation behavior across oRPC, Inngest, Elysia if considered, OpenTelemetry/OTLP, and HyperDX. The child maps vendor envelopes, RAWR runtime outcomes, harness/boundary records, telemetry projection, product observability residuals, public/native host error mapping, and layer-disagreement projection rules, then recommends the next executable proof slice. It adds no runtime behavior proof and does not imply production migration readiness, durable async semantics, product observability, RuntimeCatalog persistence, native host telemetry/error mapping, public API/DX law, or final structure/Nx/generator ratchet.

entries.source: Phase Three child 4 layer-disagreement failure observation proof
entries.oracle: Contained proof that representative oRPC and Inngest boundary responses preserve layer-specific disagreement inside the mini-runtime lab instead of collapsing into false-green readiness. The focused oracle proves oRPC HTTP 200 can carry a RAWR failure body, Inngest HTTP 206 StepRun can carry a RAWR payload failure, boundary records distinguish HTTP/protocol/payload status, telemetry projection preserves runtime/adapter/harness/boundary failure attributes before export, OTLP export failure remains observation-plane only, and the migration/control-plane packet keeps run/source/name/export summary correlation without becoming status, persistence, placement, or product observability authority. This does not imply production migration readiness, real Elysia serving, durable async semantics, live HyperDX product visibility, RuntimeCatalog persistence, native host telemetry/error mapping, public API/DX law, or final structure/Nx/generator ratchet.

entries.source: Phase Three child 5 contained Elysia host passage
entries.oracle: Planned contained proof slice for a real Elysia host/mount/request layer around the mini-runtime server boundary. The child should determine whether a real Elysia app can receive a real Request, delegate through the contained server runtime path, preserve host/oRPC-if-crossed/RAWR runtime/harness/boundary/telemetry/control-plane layers, and fence production HTTP readiness, OpenAPI/product policy, auth/logging, native host telemetry/error mapping, production migration readiness, and final public API/DX law.

entries.source: Phase Three child 1 live-runtime-passage scope and claim ledger
entries.oracle: The child 1 workstream maps live-runtime-passage dimensions, proof ceilings, residual containers, and the first executable proof-slice recommendation for DRA disposition. It adds no runtime behavior proof, does not promote Phase Two lab evidence to production readiness, and does not implement production migration, durable async semantics, product observability, RuntimeCatalog persistence, final public API/DX law, or final structure/Nx/generator ratchet.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 22, 23.3, and 29; DRA research program workflow Runtime Telemetry + HyperDX Observation cycle
entries.oracle: Lab-contained telemetry projection/export takes already-redacted mini-runtime process, provider, and catalog records, preserves useful runtime identity fields, rejects live handles/raw secrets/descriptor bodies/provider values/runtime access objects, builds stable OTLP trace payloads, and emits through the explicit local exporter path. Local HyperDX/OTLP ingest smoke is supporting lab observation only and does not prove production observability policy, persistence, deployment placement, native host telemetry, dashboard/query policy, or durable async semantics.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 22, 23.3, 23.5, and 29; residual from audit.telemetry.hyperdx-observation
entries.oracle: Product observability policy, dashboard/query policy, retention, alerting, correlation naming, production OpenTelemetry bootstrap, persisted RuntimeCatalog storage, deployment/control-plane placement, native Elysia/oRPC/Inngest host telemetry, durable async run history, platform secret-store precedence, and arbitrary free-form diagnostic string redaction remain unresolved beyond the contained telemetry projection/export proof.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 15.7, 22.2, 22.3, 23.5, 24.2, and 27; DRA research program workflow Migration/Control-Plane Observation cycle
entries.oracle: A lab-contained, non-persistent migration/control-plane observation packet can summarize an already-safe deployment handoff, in-memory runtime catalog snapshot, redacted telemetry records, and export status for migration review; preserve app/run/trace/correlation identity; record placement candidates only as candidate facts; reject widened descriptor tables, executable closures, live handles, runtime access objects, provider values, app id drift, and telemetry run id drift; strip raw keyed secrets from reportable attributes; and avoid copying OTLP payload bodies or export response bodies. This does not prove RuntimeCatalog persistence, control-plane storage, placement policy, deployment orchestration, production telemetry/queryability, dashboard semantics, durable async history, arbitrary DLP, or production migration readiness.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 22.3, 23.5, 24.2, and 27; residual from audit.migration.control-plane-observation
entries.oracle: Production RuntimeCatalog storage backend, indexing, retention, rehydration, exact persistence format, control-plane service/package topology, deployment placement policy, orchestration, product observability/query/dashboard/alerting semantics, production OpenTelemetry bootstrap, native host telemetry, durable async run history, platform secret-store precedence, production config source order, and arbitrary free-form diagnostic string DLP remain unresolved beyond the contained observation packet proof.

entries.source: DRA runtime-realization research program closeout
entries.oracle: The bounded DRA research program sequence can close as coordination continuity only after workstreams 0-11 are submitted, proof categories remain unchanged, and residual decision packets stay authority-homed. Closeout is not runtime proof, production runtime readiness, production migration readiness, or an accepted architecture decision.

entries.source: User control input and runtime spec lock authority section 29
entries.oracle: The repo-pinned canonical runtime realization spec must match the external locked spec snapshot before future proof promotion; replacing the stale repo copy and manifest hash is authority hygiene, not runtime proof.

entries.source: M2 core-spine audit P1
entries.oracle: Older non-authoritative runtime/effect documents must be subordinated before migration agents rely on indexed docs; this is source hygiene, not a type-spine proof.
